name: Salto del Duran -- Pla d'Urgell
region_intro: Hiking around Mollerussa reveals a landscape shaped by the Urgell Canal, transforming historically arid terrain into fertile agricultural plains. The region is characterized by generally flat and regular terrain, with the Serra plateau offering elevated views and its highest point, Fita Alta, considered a prominent hill. This area features an intricate system of irrigation ponds, ditches, and extensive agricultural lands. Best hiking trails around Mollerussa The most popular hiking route is Salto del Duran -- Pla d'Urgell, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a ditch of the Urgell Canal, leading to the Salt del Duran waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Third Canal of Urgell Path – Salt del Duran Waterfall loop from Mollerussa, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This route offers views of the canal and surrounding agricultural fields, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Local hikers also love the Fita Alta Summit loop from Miralcamp, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through the Serra plateau to the Fita Alta hill, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Mollerussa is defined by the extensive Urgell Canal network, agricultural plains, and the gentle elevations of the Serra plateau. The region offers more than 30 routes, providing options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Mollerussa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Mollerussa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa's hiking terrain is generally flat and regular, largely shaped by the Urgell Canal. While historically arid, the canal has transformed it into fertile agricultural plains. You'll find gentle elevations, particularly on the Serra plateau, making most routes accessible for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there many easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Mollerussa offers a good selection of easy trails. Out of the over 30 routes available, 17 are classified as easy. The gentle relief of the area makes excursions enjoyable and suitable for all ages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17547430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Third Canal of Urgell Path – Second Bridge loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.8 km path along the canal.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Mollerussa?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Mollerussa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Third Canal of Urgell Path – Salt del Duran Waterfall loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13 km trail, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fita Alta Summit loop from Miralcamp&lt;/a&gt;, a 8.9 km route to the highest point of the Serra plateau.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the Salt del Duran Waterfall is a notable attraction along the Urgell Canal. You can visit it on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931116513/salto-del-duran-pla-d-urgell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Duran -- Pla d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt; trail. This 4.5-meter waterfall is a popular spot for hikers and cyclists alike. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3776964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Second Machine&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Beyond the Urgell Canal itself, you can explore the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, recognized as Catalonia's largest pond, offering significant biodiversity and birdwatching opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt; provides a relaxing spot with a small pond. You can also hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fita Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point of the Serra plateau, for elevated views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mollerussa?
A: The hiking routes in Mollerussa are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene paths along the Urgell Canal, the flat and accessible terrain, and the opportunity to explore the unique agricultural landscapes.

Q: Is Mollerussa a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Mollerussa is an excellent choice for family-friendly hikes due to its generally flat and gentle terrain. Many routes are suitable for all ages and fitness levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt; is also an idyllic spot for families, featuring a picnic area and a small pond with ducks and turtles.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Mollerussa?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Mollerussa region, especially on the canal paths and agricultural tracks. It's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or wildlife habitats like the Ivars i Vila-sana Pond, and to clean up after them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa's climate, with its historically arid but now fertile landscape, makes it suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails. Summers can be warm, but the shaded paths along the Urgell Canal provide some relief. Winters are typically mild, allowing for comfortable walks.

Q: Are there any short, easy loops for a quick walk?
A: Yes, for a shorter, easy loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40308572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Third Canal of Urgell Path loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;. This route is approximately 3.1 km long and can be completed in under an hour, offering a pleasant stroll along the canal.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Mollerussa area?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes available around Mollerussa, offering a diverse range of options for exploring the region's unique landscape. These routes cater to both easy and moderate ability levels.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the trails?
A: Hiking in Mollerussa offers views primarily of extensive agricultural plains, particularly fruit trees, corn, olive trees, and cereals, alongside the intricate network of the Urgell Canal and its irrigation ponds. The Serra plateau provides slightly elevated perspectives, with the Fita Alta offering the highest viewpoints in the area.
tips:
Popular destination for hiking or cycling among locals. It is a beautiful four-meter waterfall that we found in the third drought of the Urgell canal.

For years it provided electricity to a large company in the town of Mollerussa, which was the engine of its economy.
